accepted.reason: Geohash bit-interleaving encode/decode loops are compact geospatial numeric kernels.
Prototype compression and nearest-distance classification are practical lightweight ML kernels.
Vector algebra, segment intersection, and reflection primitives provide reusable geometry optimization patterns.
Binomial coefficient accumulation is a concise combinatorics kernel with simple loop arithmetic.
Topological dynamic path extraction from DAGs is high-value graph optimization logic.
Spinlock-style circular insertion and position-tracking loops are strong iterative state-update kernels.
Dense/sparse stochastic logistic updates provide realistic iterative optimization workloads.
Moller-Trumbore ray-triangle intersection loops are classic 3D geometry acceleration kernels.

skipped.reason: Mostly C-wrapper data-structure glue (external calg dependency) with low standalone Python-kernel value.
Heavy OpenCV/SciPy image pipeline integration; less suitable as a concise standalone GRPO conversion target.
